title: aster lanceolatus subsp. simplex
intro: Aster lanceolatus subsp. simplex, also known as the White Wood's Aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, such as reducing stress, promoting digestion, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Aster lanceolatus subsp. simplex can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed orally to harness its potential medicinal properties.
